cards.def: The ability to make good judgments and quick decisions, typically in a particular domain.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Display sharp business acumen"
cards.context: Often used to describe a brilliant professional, investor, or leader whose exceptional insight leads to success where others fail.
cards.vis: A group of incredibly sharp-witted business executives wearing giant, pointed 'acute' triangles on their heads, flawlessly predicting stock trends.

cards.def: A person or thing that is the direct opposite of someone or something else.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "The absolute antithesis of democracy"
cards.context: Used in sentences featuring a sharp contrast, often signaled by shift words like 'whereas' or 'on the other hand' to contrast two opposing concepts.
cards.vis: An angry student writing a thesis paper, while a giant, fiery clone stands directly opposite them, aggressively tearing the papers to shreds.

cards.def: Attribute something to a cause, person, or period.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Ascribe the success to hard work"
cards.context: Appears in causal reasoning contexts, where an effect or a historical artifact is attributed to a specific origin or author.
cards.vis: An ancient scribe desperately engraving a pharaoh's name onto a giant stone tablet to make sure everyone credits him for building the pyramids.

cards.def: Unable to think clearly; confused or perplexed.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Left completely befuddled by the instructions"
cards.context: Used to describe researchers or individuals confronted with contradictory data, complex paradoxes, or inexplicable phenomena.
cards.vis: A confused Elmer Fudd scratching his head so hard his brain turns into a muddy, swirling puddle while looking at a map upside down.

cards.def: Deliberately avoid using; abstain from.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Eschew conventional career paths"
cards.context: Typically describes an ascetic, purist, or radical individual who intentionally rejects mainstream luxuries, vices, or traditional methods.
cards.vis: A health nut violently sneezing whenever someone offers them a plate of greasy junk food, diving over a table to avoid taking a bite.

cards.def: Intended for or likely to be understood by only a small number of people with a specialized knowledge or interest.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Fascinated by esoteric religious rituals"
cards.context: Contrasted against ideas that are widely popular or accessible, signaling an academic discipline or hobby that is obscure and highly specialized.
cards.vis: A secret society of scholars wearing neon wizard robes, huddling in a dark corner to read a glowing, levitating textbook written in ancient emojis.

cards.def: Tending to avoid commitment or self-revelation, especially by responding indirectly.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Give an evasive answer to the press"
cards.context: Used to characterize politicians, suspects, or witnesses who deliberately give vague, unhelpful responses to direct questioning.
cards.vis: A tiny politician running a literal obstacle course on stage, ducking and diving under massive swinging boulders labeled 'YES' or 'NO'.

cards.def: Show or declare that someone is not guilty of wrongdoing.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "New DNA evidence will exculpate the suspect"
cards.context: Appears in legal or accusatory contexts where newly discovered evidence or testimonies clear a wrongly accused party of blame.
cards.vis: A judge swinging a massive gavel that transforms a crying prisoner's orange jumpsuit into a pristine, white angel outfit, freeing him instantly.

cards.def: Make an action or process happen sooner or be accomplished more quickly.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Expedite the shipping process"
cards.context: Used when bureaucratic red tape is cleared, or catalysts are introduced to accelerate a sluggish process or development.
cards.vis: An expert delivery driver wearing rocket boots, blasting through stacks of slow-moving paperwork to slap a package onto a customer's doorstep.

cards.def: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail; very concerned about matters of cleanliness.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Maintain fastidious grooming habits"
cards.context: Describes critics, artists, or researchers whose extreme attention to minor details makes them incredibly difficult to please.
cards.vis: A hyper-neat chef using a pair of microscopic tweezers to align every single sesame seed on a burger bun, wearing a hazmat suit to prevent dust.

cards.def: Pretend to be affected by a feeling, injury, or state.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Feign illness to stay home from school"
cards.context: Often used when an actor, politician, or predator fabricates an emotion (like surprise or innocence) to deceive others.
cards.vis: A soccer player dramatically rolling across the entire stadium field, clutching his knee in fake agony, while winking directly at the camera.

cards.def: Attempting to avoid notice or attention, typically because of guilt or a belief that discovery would lead to trouble; secretive.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Cast a furtive glance at the test paper"
cards.context: Describes underhanded, surreptitious actions like stealing glances, passing illicit notes, or moving stealthily to avoid detection.
cards.vis: A sly raccoon wearing a black trench coat and sunglasses, tip-toeing on its hind legs while carrying a glowing diamond out of a museum.

cards.def: Hinder or impede the movement or progress of.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Bad weather will hamper rescue efforts"
cards.context: Indicates an external obstacle, constraint, or disadvantage that slows down or reduces the effectiveness of an operation.
cards.vis: An Olympic sprinter trying to run a 100m dash while completely trapped inside a giant, overflowing wicker laundry hamper.

cards.def: Absolutely necessary; essential.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "An indispensable member of the team"
cards.context: Identifies an element, tool, or person whose absence would cause an entire project, machine, or system to collapse completely.
cards.vis: A giant glowing golden cog in the center of a clockwork machine; when a hand tries to pull it out, the entire universe starts to freeze.

cards.def: A passionate expression of grief or sorrow; to mourn.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Lament the loss of a golden opportunity"
cards.context: Used when commentators, authors, or citizens deeply regret the decline of cultural standards, loss of life, or missed historical chances.
cards.vis: A movie director weeping hysterically into a bucket of popcorn because his highly anticipated movie had an incredibly lame ending.

cards.def: Nearsighted; lacking imagination, foresight, or intellectual insight.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A myopic focus on short-term profits"
cards.context: Criticizes leaders or policies that only look at immediate rewards while completely ignoring disastrous long-term consequences.
cards.vis: A corporate CEO wearing glasses so thick they look like microscopes, staring intensely at a single penny on the floor while a giant meteor approaches.

cards.def: Feeling or appearing casually calm and relaxed; not displaying anxiety, interest, or enthusiasm.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Adopt a nonchalant attitude toward danger"
cards.context: Describes individuals who remain remarkably unbothered, cool, or indifferent in situations that normally provoke panic or excitement.
cards.vis: A secret agent calmly sipping a hot cup of tea and reading a magazine while the building behind him explodes in massive orange fireballs.

cards.def: Existing only in part; incomplete; having a liking for or prejudice toward.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A partial judge cannot deliver justice"
cards.context: Can mean fragmented/incomplete, but on the GRE, it frequently describes biased, non-objective judges, reports, or perspectives.
cards.vis: A referee wearing a team's jersey underneath his uniform, blowing his whistle to award points based on which team he likes best.

cards.def: Engaged in, involving, or reflecting deep or serious thought.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Stare out the window in a pensive mood"
cards.context: Portrays a melancholic or deeply reflective intellectual contemplating a difficult problem, philosophical dilemma, or past event.
cards.vis: A philosopher chewing deeply on the cap of his pen, staring out a rainy window so intensely that his thoughts manifest as floating equations.

cards.def: Be a sign or warning that something, especially something momentous or calamitous, is likely to happen.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Dark clouds that portend a violent storm"
cards.context: Appears alongside omens, indicators, or early data points that forecast future disasters, economic downturns, or historical shifts.
cards.vis: A fortune teller staring into a crystal ball that displays a plunging stock market line graph, gasping in horror at the poor trend.

cards.def: Of or concerning a province; unsophisticated or narrow-minded.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Dismay at their provincial attitudes"
cards.context: Used disparagingly to describe viewpoints or people lacking cosmopolitan sophistication, worldly experience, or openness to new ideas.
cards.vis: A villager terrified of a smartphone, screaming and trying to attack the glowing device with a pitchfork thinking it's black magic.

cards.def: Involving or limited to basic principles; immature, undeveloped, or basic.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Possess only a rudimentary knowledge of physics"
cards.context: Contrasted against advanced, sophisticated, or highly evolved systems, indicating the earliest, primitive stages of a technology or idea.
cards.vis: A caveman trying to build a spaceship out of raw tree logs and mud, hitting it with a rock while grunting in frustration.

cards.def: Producing good effects; beneficial (especially with reference to something unwelcome or unpleasant).
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "A salutary lesson in fiscal responsibility"
cards.context: Describes a harsh, painful, or difficult experience (like a severe critique or failure) that ultimately yields highly beneficial, educational results.
cards.vis: A doctor forcing a patient to drink a glass of foul, bitter green sludge, which immediately makes the patient glow with radiant health.

cards.def: Divide by cutting or slicing, especially suddenly and forcibly; break off a relationship.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Sever diplomatic ties with the rogue nation"
cards.context: Used when nations abruptly end alliances, or when individuals completely cut off communication, ties, or connections with others.
cards.vis: A massive, sharp guillotine blade dropping down to cleanly slice a thick, iron chain linking two nations' flags together.

cards.def: Insubstantial; deeply insult or treat with disrespect.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Feel deeply insulted by the perceived slight"
cards.context: On the GRE, this is almost always used as a noun or verb representing a deliberate social snub, insult, or show of disrespect.
cards.vis: A wealthy aristocrat turning his nose up and completely walking past a person offering a handshake, treating them like they are invisible.

cards.def: Sleepy; drowsy; inducing drowsiness.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Lulled into a somnolent state by the lecture"
cards.context: Describes boring prose, repetitive music, or exhausting heat waves that put audiences or observers into a literal or figurative slumber.
cards.vis: A professor reading an incredibly boring textbook aloud, causing the students, the chairs, and even the classroom clock to fall asleep.

cards.def: Enduring pain and hardship without showing one's feelings or complaining.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Maintain a stoic endurance throughout the crisis"
cards.context: Portrays individuals who bear extreme grief, physical agony, or immense misfortune with an unyielding, unemotional poker face.
cards.vis: A warrior carved out of solid granite standing completely still and unbothered while lightning bolts and heavy rain crash all around him.

cards.def: Take the place of a person or thing previously in authority or use.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "The new regulation will supersede old laws"
cards.context: Appears when outdated scientific theories, obsolete technologies, or repealed legal frameworks are officially replaced by modern ones.
cards.mnem: SUPER-SEDE -> Super seed replaces old plant.
cards.vis: A glowing, futuristic cybernetic smartphone physically kicking an old, dusty rotary telephone off a table to take its place.

cards.def: Attempt to sell something, typically by pestering people in an aggressive or bold manner; highly praise.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Tout the health benefits of a new diet"
cards.context: Describes marketers, advocates, or enthusiastic supporters aggressively promoting or exaggerating the virtues of a product or idea.
cards.vis: A salesman with a massive megaphone screaming at the top of his lungs about how his magical snake oil can cure baldness instantly.

cards.def: Decrease in vigor, power, or size; become weaker.
cards.phrase: 💡 Daily life: "Watch his political influence begin to wane"
cards.context: Used to show a decline in intensity, authority, popularity, or strength—frequently contrasted with growth words like 'wax' or 'surge'.
cards.vis: A glowing superhero losing his luminescent neon light, slowly shrinking down into a normal, tired guy as a dark rain cloud covers him.
